Inspected the candidate payload builders in [ui_bridge_payloads.py](C:/Users/alber/OneDrive/문서/GitHub/experiment2/.local/upstream/jakal-flow/src/jakal_flow/ui_bridge_payloads.py#L674) and left the repo unchanged. The full-detail path already funnels log data through a single `DetailLogSnapshot` and threads that snapshot into `build_activity_lines` and `bottom_panel_payload`, so the reported repeated-read bottleneck is not safely actionable without turning this into a speculative refactor. That behavior is already protected by the upstream tests in [test_ui_bridge.py](C:/Users/alber/OneDrive/문서/GitHub/experiment2/.local/upstream/jakal-flow/tests/test_ui_bridge.py#L3199), which assert the relevant log tails are read once on a full-detail cache miss.

Verification ran clean from the managed repo root with `python -m pytest`: 22 tests passed in 124.69s. The managed repository worktree is still clean.
